seed.description: Discrimination test: dialogue alternates between genuine explicit awareness signals and superficially-similar one-off task instructions. Tests whether the agent correctly distinguishes 'this is a long-term preference' from 'this is just for the current task'.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Don't include implementation details in docs — that's a rule for everything I write, not just this one.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — no implementation details in any docs from now on.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Don't include the auth section in this one — covered separately.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Skipping auth section for this doc.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Always use OpenAPI 3.1 style. That's our standard everywhere.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — OpenAPI 3.1 as the standard for all API docs.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Always include a changelog at the top — for this version specifically.
I prefer examples over prose explanations. Always show code examples first, then text.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Got it — examples-first, prose second, as a default.

dialogue_rounds.user_message: Just give me a sketch first.
Be more concrete here — abstract phrasing isn't useful.
Going forward, never use the word 'simply' or 'just' in any docs — it's condescending.
dialogue_rounds.agent_reply: Confirmed — banning 'simply' and 'just' from all docs.

qa_items.question: What rules apply to everything I write going forward?
qa_items.tester_hint_answer: No implementation details in docs, OpenAPI 3.1 style as standard, examples before prose, ban 'simply' and 'just' as condescending.
